iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Madras
iits.terrain: An expansive, low-lying coastal plain covered by a thick, lush canopy of tropical dry evergreen forest.
iits.nearest_railway: Puratchi Thalaivar Dr. M.G. Ramachandran Central Railway Station

weather.summer: Oppressively hot, sultry, and heavily humid, with daytime ambient temperatures frequently soaring between 35°C and 42°C, intensified by the coastal marine breeze.
weather.winter: Extremely pleasant, brief, and warm with sticky humidity dropping significantly; night temperatures mellow down comfortably between 22°C and 30°C.
weather.monsoon: Subject to violent, torrential downpours driven primarily by the Northeast retreating monsoon from October to December, causing high humidity and localized waterlogging.

iits.fests: Shaastra (a high-octane, ISO-certified student-led annual technical festival) and Saarang (a massive cultural fiesta celebrating music, dance, and fine arts).
iits.night_life_culture: Vibrant in-campus night culture with spots like Gurunath, nocturnal technical hackathons, and multi-cuisine late-night eateries.
iits.campus_personality: Nature-centric, serene, and deeply traditional yet technologically progressive; shares borders with Guindy National Park, meaning deer and blackbucks roam freely.
iits.notable_feature: Houses the country's first university-driven Research Park (IITM Research Park) and leads the nation in patent filings.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi
iits.terrain: A highly optimized, compact urban terrain located on flat land along the southern edge of the Delhi Ridge.

weather.summer: Intensely hot, dry, and blistering with temperatures reaching extreme ranges of 38°C to 45°C, frequently accompanied by dusty wind conditions known locally as 'Loo'.
weather.winter: Severely cold, crisp, and biting with daytime temperatures skyrocketing downwards to ranges between 5°C and 15°C, heavily blanketed by dense seasonal smog and early morning radiation fog.
weather.monsoon: Humid and brief rainy spell from July to September, bringing heavy localized thunderstorms that offer sudden relief from the extreme northern heatwaves.

iits.fests: Tryst (a premier technical festival showcasing cutting-edge engineering modules and hackathons) and Rendezvous (one of Northern India's most popular cultural fests featuring star-studded pro-shows).
iits.night_life_culture: Highly energetic urban nightlife with dynamic late-night institutional cafes (SDA market directly opposite the gate) and around-the-clock lab accessibility.
iits.campus_personality: Fast-paced, hyper-competitive, entrepreneurial, and densely structured with interconnected modern walking walkways.
iits.notable_feature: Unmatched startup pedigree and unicorn generation ecosystem situated in India's political and startup heartland.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay
iits.terrain: A picturesque, undulating hilly landscape nestled tightly along the tranquil banks of Powai and Vihar lakes.
iits.nearest_metro_station_name: IIT Powai Metro Station (Line 6 / Nearby Jagruti Nagar on Blue Line 1)

weather.summer: Moderately hot but excessively sticky and humid, keeping ambient comfort levels low with steady coastal temperatures varying between 32°C and 38°C.
weather.winter: Extremely short, mild, and highly comfortable seasonal transition where humidity drops slightly, offering cool evening lake breezes and stable daytime ranges of 18°C to 30°C.
weather.monsoon: Characterized by relentless, torrential, and heavy precipitation from June to September, turning the entire campus hyper-green while introducing persistent lake flooding vulnerabilities.

iits.fests: Techfest (celebrated globally as Asia's largest science and technological exposition) and Mood Indigo (the legendary, massive cultural festival attracting the biggest international artists and collegiate crowds).
iits.night_life_culture: Lively and diverse nocturnal subculture; features continuous open-air theater events, cultural practices, and long midnight strolls around lakeside pathways.
iits.campus_personality: Cosmopolitan, creatively liberated, and highly expressive; uniquely flanked by Powai Lake on one side and Vihar Lake on the other.
iits.notable_feature: Hosts 'Mood Indigo', Asia's grandest college cultural festival, and maintains top-tier industry-funded research configurations.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur
iits.terrain: An immense, uninterrupted flat landscape tracking across the fertile Indo-Gangetic alluvial plains. The campus layout features vast open fields, wide straight avenues, massive agricultural research testing blocks, and a fully functional institutional airfield runway configuration.

weather.summer: Scorching, brutally dry, and desert-like continental conditions where daylight heatwaves consistently breach scales from 40°C to 46°C under blinding, cloudless skies.
weather.winter: Severely cold, foggy, and damp with temperatures dropping sharply to 4°C to 12°C, causing days of low visibility that obscure the campus aviation fields.
weather.monsoon: Substantial, heavy downpours occurring mid-year, which temporarily wash over the dry dusty plains and cool down the intense regional soil beds.

iits.fests: Techkriti (a rigorous international technical and entrepreneurial festival focusing on core innovation) and Antaragni (a celebrated cultural extravaganza with deep traditions in debating, rock music, and theater).
iits.night_life_culture: Intense academic and coding nocturnal life, backed by the legendary open-all-night 'Main Canteen' culture and residential student group debates.
iits.campus_personality: Purely academic, research-intensive, collaborative, and distinctively self-contained like a sovereign educational township.
iits.notable_feature: Possesses its own fully operational airstrip, national flight laboratory, and a unique flight testing aircraft for aerospace programs.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur
iits.terrain: A sprawling, flat industrial plateau landscape composed of hard, distinct red laterite soil. The sheer geographic scale creates a self-contained layout filled with massive residential sectors, historical brick structures, and extensive agricultural research lands.
iits.nearest_airport: Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport (Kolkata)

weather.summer: Oppressively hot and highly humid with temperatures averaging between 38°C and 44°C, creating a heavily damp and energy-draining environment before monsoon breakout.
weather.winter: Pleasantly cool, refreshing, and crisp with clear skies and comfortable ranges from 10°C to 22°C, serving as the ideal period for massive outdoor inter-hall sporting matches.
weather.monsoon: Subject to violent tropical depressions and very heavy cyclical rainfall stemming directly from the Bay of Bengal, resulting in long, continuous weekly downpours.

iits.fests: Kshitij (the largest student-run techno-management festival in Asia with premium global design exhibitions) and Spring Fest (a massive, multi-day winter cultural carnival celebrating art, drama, and global music).
iits.night_life_culture: Unique, deep-rooted 'Hall Culture' where massive inter-hall competitions foster fierce camaraderie, supplemented by late-night tea stalls like Cheddis.
iits.campus_personality: Historic, monumental, vintage, and profoundly tightly knit due to its isolated geography away from major metropolitan centers.
iits.notable_feature: The oldest and geographically largest IIT campus in India; uniquely features its own institutional Medical and Law Schools.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee
iits.terrain: A perfectly flat, highly stylized landscape located in the immediate sub-Himalayan foothill basin next to the Upper Ganges Canal. The terrain is characterized by immaculate, manicured gardens, wide symmetrical streets, and majestic white-washed British colonial architecture.

weather.summer: Abruptly hot and dry under direct, unshaded mountain valley sunshine, with daytime temperatures fluctuating from 36°C to 42°C before evening relief breezes kick in.
weather.winter: Crisp, beautiful, and intensely cold with fresh Himalayan wind flows driving morning temperatures down to 5°C to 15°C, requiring heavy woolens.
weather.monsoon: Subject to heavy, sudden foothill downpours and mountain storms from July to September, which rapidly fill the local river systems and clean the landscape.

iits.fests: Cognizance (a premium national technical festival focused on addressing global civil and digital infrastructure challenges) and Thomso (a deeply historic, massive cultural fest known for its royal artistic themes).
iits.night_life_culture: Highly cohesive community lifestyle with regular midnight musical jams, student activity center hubs, and late-night campus cafes.
iits.campus_personality: Majestic, historically rich, and structurally striking; showcases British colonial architecture integrated with cutting-edge civic frameworks.
iits.notable_feature: Originally founded as the Thomason College of Civil Engineering in 1847, it is the oldest technical institution in Asia.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad
iits.terrain: A vast, open semi-arid Deccan plateau terrain. The landscape is flat, rocky, and heavily structured with dramatic futuristic buildings, concrete plazas, and a unique grid layout designed around specialized heat-reducing architectural shells.
iits.nearest_metro_station_name: Miyapur Metro Station (Red Line - Located 35km away towards Hyderabad city center)

weather.summer: Extremely dry, harsh, and punishingly hot with temperatures averaging 38°C to 44°C, aggravated by the rocky, heat-absorbent nature of the local terrain.
weather.winter: Extremely pleasant, dry, and cool with clear blue skies and refreshing nights dropping to comfortable ranges of 15°C to 25°C.
weather.monsoon: Moderate and highly erratic precipitation patterns that turn the dry scrubby landscape fields briefly green without heavy waterlogging issues.

iits.fests: Elan & nVision (a massive combined techno-cultural festival featuring extensive electronic media arts, competitive coding arenas, and multi-genre musical stages).
iits.night_life_culture: Tech-centric nocturnal ecosystem featuring late-night coding sprints, innovation hub development labs, and modern, well-connected dynamic food courts.
iits.campus_personality: Futuristic, ultra-modern, architectural marvel; heavily defined by its visually stunning, avant-garde, radiant Japanese-style structural design.
iits.notable_feature: Maintains exceptional strategic academic collaborations with Japanese universities and pioneers research in 5G/6G, AI, and EV tech.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar
iits.terrain: A beautiful, flat riverside terrain rolling gently into the western banks of the Sabarmati River. The property features terraced landscapes, massive eco-green ravines, earthen walking trails, and modern brick masonry buildings designed to blend with the natural riverbanks.
iits.nearest_metro_station_name: Sector-1 Metro Station (Ahmedabad-Gandhinagar Metro Yellow Line)

weather.summer: Blistering, hyper-arid, and intensely scorching summer seasons where daytime records peak violently from 40°C to 46°C, creating high thermal stress.
weather.winter: Delightfully cool, crisp, and completely dry with a cold northern breeze settling over the river basin, maintaining perfect ranges of 12°C to 26°C.
weather.monsoon: Light to moderate seasonal showers from July to September that revitalize the arid riverside greenery without heavy industrial disruptions.

iits.fests: Amalthea (India's premier annual student-run technical summit focusing strictly on industry innovations and corporate tech talks) and Blithchron (a vibrant, highly interactive cultural dance and music festival).
iits.night_life_culture: Socially progressive, intellectually open atmosphere where student clubs stay active until the early hours alongside scenic, lighted riverside promenades.
iits.campus_personality: Eco-friendly, highly inclusive, aesthetic, and non-hierarchical; explicitly engineered to facilitate maximum student-faculty interaction.
iits.notable_feature: First 5-star GRIHA LD rated green campus in India; renowned for its flagship, mandatory 5-week Foundation Programme for freshman students.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Ropar
iits.terrain: A lush, flat, agricultural riverine plain located along the wetlands of the Sutlej River. The soil is rich and silty, creating an environmentally tranquil landscape surrounded by local farms, wide rural fields, and modern white-pillar infrastructure facilities.

weather.summer: Intensely hot, sticky, and heavily humid during peak summer months, with temperature ranges hovering uncomfortably around 38°C to 44°C.
weather.winter: Bitingly cold, frosty, and severe continental winter weather where morning values regularly plunge to 4°C to 14°C, surrounded by dense northern field fog.
weather.monsoon: Heavy, localized sub-Himalayan monsoon patterns that bring massive cloudbursts and deep watering to the surrounding regional wetland basins.

iits.fests: Advitiya (a fast-evolving national technical symposium promoting young developers and hardware projects) and Zeitgeist (the premier annual cultural festival of the region, rich in Punjabi folk arts and modern indie pop).
iits.night_life_culture: Tight-knit residential community life where late-night activities revolve around academic group hubs, hostel common lounges, and night utility canteens.
iits.campus_personality: Clean, highly organized, culturally expressive, and peaceful; features an architectural layout reflecting modern interpretations of classic Punjabi motifs.
iits.notable_feature: Consistently matches or outperforms older IITs in global research citation metrics and houses a premier Agriculture-IoT Hub (AWADH).

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Patna
iits.terrain: A completely flat, vast landscape situated on the historic alluvial plains of the Gangetic basin in Bihta. The terrain features deep agricultural soils, an open landscape layout, and growing modern institutional structures engineered to scale into self-contained town systems.

weather.summer: Sweltering, thick, and highly muggy with intense tropical heat waves pushing midday temperature peaks between 38°C and 44°C.
weather.winter: Very cold, gloomy, and heavily damp due to cold waves blowing across the Gangetic plains, dragging temperatures down to 6°C to 16°C with persistent fog layers.
weather.monsoon: Heavy, continuous monsoon precipitation running from June to September, filling the regional water tables and washing over the open flat campus blocks.

sports_facilities: Indoor Table Tennis and Carrom Rooms
iits.fests: Celesta (a dynamic national-level techno-management festival focusing on competitive programming and robotics) and Anwesha (the grand annual cultural fest known for its mass student participation and artistic exhibitions).
iits.night_life_culture: Steadily evolving nocturnal culture focusing heavily on algorithmic coding competitions, placement prep groups, and late-night hostel terrace discussions.
iits.campus_personality: Disciplined, determined, culturally traditional, and rapidly evolving in its academic infrastructure footprint.
iits.notable_feature: Features state-of-the-art incubation facilities specializing in speech-to-speech translation technology and advanced materials research.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati
iits.terrain: A breathtakingly beautiful, undulating hilly valley terrain tracking right along the northern banks of the majestic Brahmaputra River. The landscape features deep internal fresh-water lakes, migratory bird sanctuaries, steep green hills, and sloping forest walks.

weather.summer: Warm but profoundly humid and wet, keeping maximum heat indexes contained between 30°C and 36°C while remaining heavy and damp throughout the season.
weather.winter: Exceedingly beautiful, chilly, and foggy with dense morning mists rising from the Brahmaputra River, creating crisp day comfort ranges of 8°C to 18°C.
weather.monsoon: Subject to exceptionally long, heavy, and intense monsoon periods stretching from May to September, keeping the entire hilly ecosystem saturated with lush vegetation.

iits.fests: Techniche (a highly regarded national techno-management festival attracting global innovators and thinkers) and Alcheringa (celebrated as one of the largest and most visually vibrant cultural fests in Northeast India).
iits.night_life_culture: Scenic, deeply reflective campus life; night-time activities embrace cultural fests, open-air movie screenings, and lakeside brainstorming sessions.
iits.campus_personality: Breathtakingly beautiful, tranquil, and ecologically abundant; flanked by the majestic Brahmaputra River and scenic internal lakes.
iits.notable_feature: Widely acknowledged as the most visually scenic and landscape-gorgeous college campus in India; features a dedicated center for Nanotechnology.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi
iits.terrain: An iconic, historic, and highly unique integrated layout situated within the massive semicircular, tree-lined township of Banaras Hindu University. The terrain is entirely flat and urban, deeply integrated with majestic heritage buildings, vast religious shrines, and dense residential avenues flanked by aged banyan trees.

weather.summer: Blistering and heavily oppressive dry continental heat wave conditions with ambient daylight temperatures routinely spiking between 40°C and 46°C.
weather.winter: Extremely chilly, foggy, and damp with morning temperature scales dropping sharply between 5°C and 13°C, heavily influenced by the adjacent Gangetic river mist.
weather.monsoon: Humid and heavy precipitation patterns from July to September that completely saturate the old historic drainage channels and bring lush green life to the township.

iits.fests: Technex (one of India's oldest premier national techno-management celebrations featuring extensive innovation modules) and Kashiyatra (a massive, vibrant cultural carnival deeply rooted in literature, music, and dramatic arts).
iits.night_life_culture: Fascinating and unique open-campus midnight culture where students gather late at Limbdi Corner, institution canteens, and local ghats for tea and brainstorming.
iits.campus_personality: Deeply traditional, culturally spiritual, and historically rich; possesses a uniquely non-walled, integrated university structure that blends old-world heritage with modern research.
iits.notable_feature: Originally founded as the Banaras Engineering College (BENCO) in 1919, it holds a monumental legacy as a pioneer of engineering education in India.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Indore
iits.terrain: A modern, highly optimized terrain located across the scenic rolling slopes of the Malwa plateau in Simrol. The campus is flat to gently undulating, designed with clean open zones, specialized landscape gardens, and massive state-of-the-art glass-and-concrete academic skyscrapers.
iits.nearest_metro_station_name: Radisson Square Metro Station (Indore Metro Yellow Line - Under Construction/Partial Routes)

weather.summer: Hot but relatively dry and manageable due to the elevated plateau breeze, with daylight summer peaks scaling between 38°C and 43°C.
weather.winter: Pleasantly crisp, refreshing, and moderately cold with clear starry skies and night-time drops lingering comfortably around 10°C to 20°C.
weather.monsoon: Moderate to heavy rainfall intervals that beautifully transform the dry semi-arid Simrol hills into an incredibly lush, green landscape.

iits.fests: Fluxus (the premier techno-cultural annual festival of central India, renowned for hosting high-profile independent artists and intense coding hackathons).
iits.night_life_culture: A highly active, clean, and disciplined indoor residential night culture where late hours are dominated by research labs and modern student dining lounges.
iits.campus_personality: Ultra-modern, highly efficient, and research-focused; structurally designed to reflect the title of being part of India's cleanest city ecosystem.
iits.notable_feature: Possesses world-class specialized research instrumentation facilities, frequently leading newer institutions in global publication metrics.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology (Indian School of Mines) Dhanbad
iits.terrain: A historic, deeply forested, and undulating rocky landscape situated right over the resource-rich Chota Nagpur Plateau. The layout features dense tropical woodlands, rolling gravel pathways, vintage British stone architecture, and specialized underground exploration mock setups.

weather.summer: Intensely hot, dry, and harsh due to the surrounding rocky terrain and industrial zone footprints, with temperatures peaking around 39°C to 45°C.
weather.winter: Crisp, biting, and highly refreshing with dry, cool winds sweeping across the plateau hills, bringing evening comfort scales down to 7°C to 15°C.
weather.monsoon: Substantial, heavy downpours that wash over the rocky mineral soils and completely clean the dense green campus tree covers.

iits.fests: NVision (a premium technical festival focused strictly on mineral extraction, core engineering, and digital systems) and Srijan (the largest socio-cultural carnival of Eastern India).
iits.night_life_culture: Rich, deeply traditional campus community night culture centered around long walks past the historic Heritage Building and discussions at late canteens.
iits.campus_personality: Vintage, proud, and heavily tightly knit; carries a profound institutional history mirroring old royal British research academies.
iits.notable_feature: Established in 1926 on the model of the Royal School of Mines, London; houses the most advanced earth science and mining laboratories in Asia.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Mandi
iits.terrain: A breathtaking, completely mountainous terrain situated deep within the dramatic Uhl River valley in Kamand. The campus is split across the steep slopes of the lower Himalayas, featuring heavy terraced pathways, high altitude suspension bridges, and rustic timber-and-stone alpine structures.

weather.summer: Incredibly pleasant and mild with clear mountain skies and ambient daylight temperatures hovering very comfortably between 20°C and 32°C.
weather.winter: Severely cold and freezing with temperatures dropping directly between 0°C and 10°C, frequently experiencing light frost and rare, picturesque snow flurries over higher valley ridges.
weather.monsoon: Highly intense, heavy sub-Himalayan rain systems that fill the roaring Uhl river rapids and cause sudden cloud mists to blanket the mountain campus blocks.

iits.fests: Xpecto (a rising national-level tech-symposium testing alpine engineering design concepts) and Exodia (a deeply scenic cultural festival celebrating music, Himalayan arts, and rock bands).
iits.night_life_culture: Cozy, quiet, and highly reflective; late-night life shifts entirely indoors into wooden hostel lounges and riverside canteens away from freezing mountain drafts.
iits.campus_personality: Eco-sensitive, serene, and adventurous; a paradise for nature enthusiasts where daily academic transit involves navigating mountain trails.
iits.notable_feature: The first IIT located entirely within the high Himalayan eco-belt, hosting unique research centers for bio-materials and landslide prediction technologies.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur
iits.terrain: A massive, sweeping desert plain landscape located on the arid outskirts of the Thar Desert. The terrain is flat, sandy, and highly exposed, engineered with state-of-the-art solar-reflective stone buildings, sand dune parks, and water-retaining geometric pathways.

weather.summer: Punishingly hot, hyper-arid, and blistering with daytime desert records routinely breaching 42°C to 48°C, intensified by dry sandy wind currents.
weather.winter: Sharp, dry, and cold with clear desert nights causing temperatures to plummet rapidly between 6°C and 16°C, requiring heavy layered clothing.
weather.monsoon: Extremely sparse, brief, and highly seasonal rain spells that barely wet the sandy terrain before evaporating instantly into the hot air.

iits.fests: Prometeo (a premium national techno-management summit focusing on sustainability and AI engineering) and Spandan (a highly expressive cultural dance, art, and fashion festival).
iits.night_life_culture: Desert night lifestyle where students enjoy long walks along illuminated concrete plazas after sunset when the harsh desert heat finally breaks down.
iits.campus_personality: Resilient, highly organized, and futuristic; structurally designed to conquer extreme desert climate conditions via green architecture.
iits.notable_feature: Maintains an incredibly advanced flagship clean-energy microgrid system and a premier center of excellence for transdisciplinary AI research.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Bhubaneswar
iits.terrain: A massive, expansive coastal plain plateau located at the foothills of the scenic Barunei hills in Argul. The landscape is flat, heavily open, and built with large symmetrical academic blocks separated by long concrete boulevards and manicured fields.

weather.summer: Extremely hot, sticky, and oppressively humid as typical of coastal plains, with daytime temperatures lingering heavily between 37°C and 43°C.
weather.winter: Extremely pleasant, mild, and highly comfortable with gentle inland winds and clear sunny days keeping temperatures stable between 15°C and 28°C.
weather.monsoon: Highly intense, violent tropical downpours driven by Bay of Bengal cyclonic systems from June to October, bringing high winds and heavy cooling showers.

iits.fests: Wissenaire (a prominent national techno-management festival focusing on aerospace and mechanical modeling) and Alma Fiesta (a highly engaging socio-cultural winter fest celebrating local art, music, and performance).
iits.night_life_culture: A peaceful, campus-focused nocturnal lifestyle where late hours involve hostel canteen group works and technical club project reviews.
iits.campus_personality: Spacious, disciplined, and calm; highly defined by its quiet setting away from urban rush, framed by the legendary Barunei hill landscapes.
iits.notable_feature: Houses an incredibly advanced, dedicated coastal climate modeling center and specializes in oceanographic engineering research modules.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu
iits.terrain: A beautiful, hilly terrain tracking along the rugged sub-Himalayan Shivalik slopes in Jagti. The property features dynamic multi-level terraces, sharp hillside curves, deep green valley lookouts, and modern hillside blocks designed to blend into the mountain ridges.

weather.summer: Hot and intensely bright during peak daytime hours with ambient valley temperature ranges scaling between 36°C and 42°C before cooling down at night.
weather.winter: Severely cold, crisp, and frosty with dry mountain winds sweeping down the Shivalik peaks, keeping values down between 4°C and 14°C.
weather.monsoon: Substantial, heavy sub-Himalayan monsoon spells that bring deep moisture clouds down into the green valley campus structures.

iits.fests: Technu (a rapidly growing technical symposium focused on regional infrastructure tech) and Rena (a vibrant cultural fest celebrating ethnic mountain arts, music, and contemporary drama).
iits.night_life_culture: Cohesive and protective indoor community life where late night activities thrive inside modern heating-equipped common rooms and hostel food hubs.
iits.campus_personality: Serene, tightly knit, and expanding; showcases a beautiful blend of peaceful mountain isolation and rapid infrastructure layout scale.
iits.notable_feature: Strategically positioned at the gateway of the Himalayas, maintaining specialized research labs for tunnel engineering and seismic hazard mitigations.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Tirupati
iits.terrain: A scenic, flat valley plain located right at the foothills of the majestic, sacred Seshachalam hill ranges in Yerpedu. The campus features an open layout with modern eco-friendly buildings integrated alongside vast stone backdrops and young green belts.

weather.summer: Extremely hot, dry, and punishingly intense under unshaded valley sun, with daytime levels peaking consistently between 38°C and 44°C.
weather.winter: Highly pleasant, mild, and comfortable with dry cooler evening breezes dropping down from the adjacent hill ranges to keep levels at 18°C to 28°C.
weather.monsoon: Subject to substantial heavy downpours during the late retreating Northeast monsoon cycles from October to December, cooling the entire stone valley down.

sports_facilities: Main Fields for Cricket and Football
iits.fests: Tirutsava (the premier annual combined techno-cultural festival of the institute, bringing together coding sprints and traditional South Indian artistic displays).
iits.night_life_culture: A peaceful and focused residential lifestyle; night hours are largely spent within the illuminated student lounges and late-night campus dining spots.
iits.campus_personality: Quiet, progressive, and highly eco-conscious; deeply defined by its panoramic views of massive holy mountain walls surrounding the perimeter.
iits.notable_feature: The first among the third-generation IITs to fully operationalize its beautiful, sustainable permanent campus infrastructure footprint.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Palakkad
iits.terrain: A beautiful, rolling, hilly valley plain situated right at the base of the massive Western Ghats along the historic Palakkad Gap. The terrain features deep red soils, natural streams, and highly modern architectural blocks built around lush tropical greenery.

weather.summer: Hot and distinctly humid with tropical ambient temperature ranges hovering between 34°C and 39°C, tempered occasionally by heavy winds through the mountain gap.
weather.winter: Mild, highly comfortable, and pleasantly warm with cooler evening mountain drafts dropping values down smoothly between 20°C and 30°C.
weather.monsoon: Subject to incredibly heavy, relentless, and torrential rainfall from the Southwest monsoon between June and September, turning the valley hyper-green.

iits.fests: Petrichor (the landmark annual techno-cultural celebration named after the refreshing scent of rain on dry earth, showcasing intense green tech models and music).
iits.night_life_culture: A quiet, tightly knit valley night scene where students gather late inside green common spaces and cozy hostel food courts away from the heavy rain blocks.
iits.campus_personality: Tranquil, nature-integrated, and highly eco-sensitive; beautifully framed by endless vistas of misty mountain peaks along the horizon.
iits.notable_feature: Strategically built adjacent to the Western Ghats biological hotspot, holding cutting-edge specialized centers for environmental and data science research.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai
iits.terrain: A modern, completely flat plains terrain located within the structural mining and steel belt layout of Kutelabhata. The campus features open, dry landscapes shifting into structured technological zones with massive industrial-grade engineering labs and highly insulated residential blocks.

weather.summer: Extremely harsh, dry, and blistering hot continental weather conditions where daylight indices scale intensely between 40°C and 46°C.
weather.winter: Pleasantly clear, dry, and mildly cold during evening hours with temperatures stabilizing comfortably between 12°C and 25°C.
weather.monsoon: Substantial and highly heavy monsoon cycles from July to September that completely saturate the open plains and clear up regional mining dust blocks.

iits.fests: Meraz (the signature annual techno-cultural celebration tracking innovative coding sprints and localized musical events).
iits.night_life_culture: A quiet, tightly focused student-centric night life concentrated around technical labs, hostel recreation rooms, and mid-night study canteens.
iits.campus_personality: Highly disciplined, industrial-focused, and steadily expanding its permanent academic footprint layout.
iits.notable_feature: Maintains highly advanced specialized partnerships with the national steel manufacturing sector and leads research in industrial automation architectures.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Dharwad
iits.terrain: A beautiful, undulating terrain located across the scenic, rolling hillocks of the Deccan transitions in Chikkamalligawad. The property highlights rich red gravel beds, sloping walking zones, and state-of-the-art modern brick-faced institutional structures engineered to harness natural winds.

weather.summer: Highly moderate and surprisingly pleasant summer seasons due to the elevated upland setting, with temperature values hovering easily between 32°C and 37°C.
weather.winter: Delightfully cool, refreshing, and crisp with clear wind flows sweeping down the hillocks, keeping evening values between 14°C and 24°C.
weather.monsoon: Subject to steady, cool, and highly refreshing monsoon rainfalls from June to September, turning the entire red-clay landscape beautifully green.

iits.fests: Parsec (the premium national-level tech-cultural festival bringing together deep learning modules and creative art workshops).
iits.night_life_culture: A peaceful and tightly knit campus night ecosystem where late-night hours are predominantly spent inside student group zones and coding hubs.
iits.campus_personality: Serene, highly academic, and eco-conscious; deeply marked by its refreshing, wind-swept climate layouts and wide panorama views.
iits.notable_feature: Houses highly progressive research laboratories specialized in data science applications and smart water infrastructure engineering.

iits.name: Indian Institute of Technology Goa
iits.terrain: An undulating, rocky laterite plateau terrain located within the scenic forest valleys of Farmagudi. The setting is dense with tropical vegetation, sloping pathways, and temporary architectural arrangements layout while scaling its permanent foundations.

weather.summer: Hot, profoundly tropical, and excessively humid with stable maritime temperatures lingering constantly between 32°C and 36°C.
weather.winter: Extremely pleasant, mild, and highly warm with crisp ocean breezes and comfortable evening ranges of 20°C to 30°C.
weather.monsoon: Subject to exceptionally heavy, relentless, and continuous coastal downpours driven by the Southwest monsoon from June to September.

iits.fests: Fluxus Goa (a rapidly expanding techno-cultural youth festival focusing on marine computing modules, rock shows, and design arts).
iits.night_life_culture: A highly collaborative, lively, and close student group night scene managing nocturnal research sprints and cultural jam sessions.
iits.campus_personality: Expressive, tight-knit, and nature-loving; uniquely characterized by its rich tropical setting away from regular tourist zones.
iits.notable_feature: Maintains highly specialized research centers focusing on marine tech computing, advanced manufacturing, and computational mechanics.

nits.name: National Institute of Technology Tiruchirappalli
